metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_277
metadata.comment:
Rationale: In this paper, the function of syb1 is investigated in the NMJ in Syb1 lew/lew mutant mice. Authors first checked if Syb1 is involved in synaptic formation. However, they did not find any evidence for this hypothesis.
In the second step, they used electrophysiology to investigate if Syb1 is involved in spontaneous or Ca2+-triggered exocytosis. They found that both spontaneous (Fig. 3) and evoked neurotransmission (Fig. 4) was reduced compared to WT mice. Then they measure the size of the readily releasable pool in Syb1 lew/lew mutant. They did not find any change compared to control. Therefore, they reject the hypothesis that Syb1 is involved in the priming of synaptic vesicles (Fig. 7).
 They found that reduction of neurotransmitter release is due to reduction Ca2+ sensitivity and cooperativity of neuromuscular synaptic transmission in Syb1lew/lew mice (Fig. 8). and elevation of external Ca2+ restores some of the phenotypes such as short-term plasticity (Fig. 9).. Experimental description: Analyses were carried out on homozygous mutants (Syb1lew/lew) and their littermate controls, which included both wild-type (WT) and heterozygous mice.
